response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and software craftsmanship background with recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, plus distributed/high-throughput experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the resume shows TypeScript/Node and DLang as primary languages with no demonstrated deep Python expertise, which is a must-have. Title is 'Senior' rather than Staff in most recent roles, and current career gap plus limited explicit distributed-systems-at-scale leadership signals make this a borderline fit worth a screening call to probe Python depth and staff-level scope.
strengths: Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generator work at re:cinq
High-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of req/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js background with TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development discipline
concerns: No demonstrated Python expertise — a stated must-have
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership
Currently on a caregiving career gap; recent re:cinq tenure was only ~6 months
